summary: chai-as-byte exfiltrates process.env and executes remote code fetched from an obfuscated endpoint
details: The package presents itself as a Pino/chai plugin but on invocation of its exported middleware initializer, lib/initializeCaller.js spawns a detached child that POSTs the entire process.env dictionary to a base64-encoded URL decoding to https://ipcheck-hashed.vercel.app/api/auth/6c1d60d35852ef0c05df. The HTTP response body is then passed to `new Function("require", response.data)` and executed with `require` injected, giving the remote server arbitrary code execution in the Node process. The destination URL is stored base64-encoded to hide the C2 host, and the package name mimics established libraries to lure installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
